name: St Andrews Beach Brewery
signature: Former horse-training stables turned sprawling brewery  -  wood-fired pizzas, Sunday roasts, acres of lawn, and the Peninsula's best brewery-day-out address
editorNote:
St Andrews Beach Brewery occupies the former stables of a racehorse training property, and the scale of the place is part of the experience. Acres of lawn roll out to the former exercise track; the brew house sits in the middle of the site; the kitchen runs wood-fired pizzas, loaded burgers, and a rotating roast on Sundays out of an open pass. Kids run between the tables, dogs run between the kids, and the whole operation has the feel of a country-estate afternoon that has accidentally turned into a brewery.

The beer is serious. The core range covers the Peninsula brewery-day hits  -  pale ale, lager, red ale, a session sour  -  and the rotating taps dip into more interesting territory when the brewers get ambitious. Paired with a wood-fired pizza and an afternoon on the lawn, it is the signature brewery stop on the Peninsula and the one that handles a group of twelve without blinking.

Book for Sunday lunch in summer. Arrive hungry. Bring a hat.

heroImage.alt: Craft brewery imagery evocative of St Andrews Beach Brewery, Mornington Peninsula
